name: CLINDUMMY_f
description: Clock dummy block - placeholder for continuous-time clock functionality
xmlExample:
<BasicBlock id="..." interfaceFunctionName="CLINDUMMY_f" blockType="c" dependsOnU="0" dependsOnT="1" simulationFunctionName="cdummy" simulationFunctionType="C_OR_FORTRAN" style="CLINDUMMY_f">
  <ScilabDouble as="exprs" height="0" width="0"/>
  <ScilabDouble as="realParameters" height="0" width="0"/>
  <ScilabDouble as="integerParameters" height="0" width="0"/>
  <ScilabInteger as="nbZerosCrossing" height="1" width="1" intPrecision="sci_int32">
    <data line="0" column="0" value="0"/>
  </ScilabInteger>
  <ScilabInteger as="nmode" height="1" width="1" intPrecision="sci_int32">
    <data line="0" column="0" value="0"/>
  </ScilabInteger>
  <ScilabDouble as="state" height="1" width="1">
    <data line="0" column="0" realPart="0.0"/>
  </ScilabDouble>
  <ScilabDouble as="dState" height="0" width="0"/>
  <mxGeometry as="geometry" x="0.0" y="-10.0" width="60.0" height="40.0"/>
</BasicBlock>
anomalies: Zero ports - no inputs or outputs
Has state with realPart=0.0 despite being a dummy block
dependsOnT=1 but no time-dependent behavior visible
criticalRules: Used internally by XCOS for clock-related operations
Block is automatically generated - users rarely place it manually
Width=60 is non-standard but required for this block
commonUses: Internal XCOS placeholder for clock synchronization
Maintaining compatibility between continuous and event systems
